title: Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple located in Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple located in Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. The temple which is dedicated to Lord Shiva is administered by Travancore Devaswom Board. During Sabarimala pilgrimage, many devotees from Kerala and other states visit the temple.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Nilakkal
topPlaces.description: Nilakkal, also spelled Nilackal, is an important base camp of Sabarimala Hindu pilgrims located in Ranni tehsil, in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. Sabarimala temple is located at a distance of 23 km (14 mi) from Nilakkal.

topPlaces.name: St. Thomas Ecumenical Church, Nilackal
topPlaces.description: Nilakkal St. Thomas Ecumenical Church is an ecumenical Christian church located in Angamoozhy, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Angamoozhy
topPlaces.description: Angamoozhy is a small village in the Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located near the Kakkad power station and the Sabarimala temple.

topPlaces.name: Mar Thoma Sleeha Syro-Malabar Church, Thulappally

image.alt: Mar Thoma Sleeha Syro-Malabar Church, Thulappally

topPlaces.name: Edakadathy
topPlaces.description: Edakadathy is a village located near Mukkoottuthara in Erumely gram panchayat, Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It is situated near the border of Kottayam district and Pathanamthitta district, but officially recorded as a part of the former.

topPlaces.name: Thulappally
topPlaces.description: Thulappally is a small village in Perunad panchayath of Ranni tehsil,Thiruvalla Revenue Division in Pathanamthitta district of Kerala state, India.

topPlaces.name: Seethathodu
topPlaces.description: Seethathodu is a village in the Konni taluk, Pathanamthitta district, state of Kerala, India, near Chittar Town. Predominantly, it is a rural region with agriculture being the most important sector.

topPlaces.name: Plappally
topPlaces.description: Plappally is a settlement located in the Goodrical range of the Ranni Forest Division in the state of Kerala, India. The place is an intersection point of Mannarakulanji–Chalakkayam state highway (NH-183) and Erumely–Thulappally–Plappally route.

items.note: Nilakkal, also spelled Nilackal, is an important base camp of Sabarimala Hindu pilgrims located in Ranni tehsil, in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. Sabarimala temple is located at a distance of 23 km (14 mi) from Nilakkal.
Nilakkal St. Thomas Ecumenical Church is an ecumenical Christian church located in Angamoozhy, Kerala, India.
Angamoozhy is a small village in the Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located near the Kakkad power station and the Sabarimala temple.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (101 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (100 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nilakkal, St. Thomas Ecumenical Church, Nilackal, Angamoozhy, Mar Thoma Sleeha Syro-Malabar Church, Thulappally, Edakadathy.
